ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the academic event associated with the notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the notification message */,
  "read_status" INTEGER /* Status indicating if the notification has been read */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the event type (e.g., Assignment, Registration) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the user role (e.g., Admin, Faculty, Student) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_exceptions" (
  "exception_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each exception */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the academic event associated with the exception */,
  "exception_date" TEXT /* Date of the exception */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the exception */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("exception_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "attendance" (
  "attendance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attendance record */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the academic event associated with the attendance */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user whose attendance is being tracked */,
  "attendance_date" TEXT /* Date of the attendance */,
  "attended" INTEGER /* Status indicating if the user attended the event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attendance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attendance_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the academic event associated with the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "attendance_count" INTEGER /* Number of users who attended the event */,
  "notification_count" INTEGER /* Number of notifications sent for the event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id")
)
